cover:
A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "LWC vs REACT: What Every Salesforce Developer Must Know Before Switching".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "LWC vs REACT" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "What Every Salesforce Developer Must Know Before Switching".

Left Column (LWC — Salesforce Native):
- Header: "LWC — Salesforce Native" in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Reactive properties with @track and @wire" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Wire adapters for SOQL and Apex — no fetch needed" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Built-in sharing + FLS enforcement" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Constrained DOM access — Locker Service / LWS" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Native state management in Summer 26" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Deployment via Salesforce CLI + metadata API" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (REACT — General Purpose Web):
- Header: "REACT — General Purpose Web" in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "useState / useReducer / useContext — explicit state" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "useEffect for all side effects including data fetching" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "No platform security — you own auth and FLS" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Full DOM access — any library, any pattern" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Zustand / Redux / Jotai — external state libraries" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "npm deploy anywhere — Vercel, Cloud Run, CDN" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.
images:
A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "LWC → REACT CONCEPT MAPPING: 14 Core Concepts Side by Side — What Changes and What Stays the Same".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "LWC → REACT CONCEPT MAPPING" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "14 Core Concepts Side by Side — What Changes and What Stays the Same".

Left Column (LWC CONCEPT):
- Header: "LWC CONCEPT" in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "@track / @api → reactive state and public props" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "@wire(getRecord) → server data binding" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "connectedCallback() → component mount lifecycle" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "template if:true / for:each → conditional + list" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Custom Events → parent-child communication" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "lightning-button, lightning-input → base components" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "SLDS → Salesforce Lightning Design System" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (REACT EQUIVALENT):
- Header: "REACT EQUIVALENT" in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "useState() → local reactive state" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "useEffect() + fetch → data fetching" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "useEffect(fn, []) → mount equivalent" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "{condition && <JSX />} and .map() → conditional + list" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Callback props → parent-child communication" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "shadcn/ui, Radix, MUI → component libraries" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Tailwind CSS → utility-first styling system" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.

A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "LWC → REACT MIGRATION CHECKLIST".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "LWC → REACT MIGRATION CHECKLIST".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "What to Port, What to Rewrite, and What to Leave Behind" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Hooks Over Wire" with a hook icon, "Explicit State" with a layers icon, "You Own Auth" with a lock icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "WRONG: Direct 1:1 Port of LWC Patterns". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "RIGHT: Rewrite with React Mental Model". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Trying to recreate @wire in React (wrong)
const [record, setRecord] = useState()
useEffect(() => {
  // faking LWC wire adapter imperatively
  getRecord({ recordId }).then(setRecord)
}, [recordId]) // missing deps, stale closures
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    // React data fetching done right
const { data, isLoading } = useSWR(
  ['case', caseId],
  () => fetchCase(caseId)
)
// SWR handles caching, revalidation,
// loading states, and error boundaries

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Replace @wire with useSWR or React Query", "Replace connectedCallback with useEffect(fn, [])", "Replace custom events with callback props", "Replace SLDS with Tailwind + shadcn/ui", "Replace Locker Service assumptions — you own DOM access now".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "LWC", "React 19", "Hooks", "Salesforce Summer 26" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
